outcome: problem=Optimize the yield of a Baumgartner 2019 Buchwald-Hartwig C-N coupling benchmark for the fixed nucleophile Benzamide. The search space is restricted to the experimentally observed conditions present in the vendored CSV. During simulation, the optimizer should not see the yield column; yields are revealed only via the dataset oracle at the human-interrupt step.

reasoning: Prioritize testing H2 (bidentate ligands) and H3 (strong bases) first due to high confidence and established literature precedent for Buchwald-Hartwig couplings. Use H1 and H4 to define catalyst and temperature boundaries. Reserve H5 for later iterations if solvent effects remain unexplained.
outcome: H1 new (active, medium): Electron-rich palladium precatalysts (e.g., Pd(OAc)2, Pd2(dba)3) will outperform electron-poor alternatives due to enhanced oxidative addition to aryl halides in Buchwald-Hartwig C-N couplings.

H2 new (active, high): Bidentate phosphine ligands (e.g., Xantphos, dppf) will provide superior yields compared to monodentate alternatives due to enhanced catalyst stability and suppressed β-hydride elimination.
H3 new (active, high): Strong, non-nucleophilic bases (NaOtBu, LiHMDS) will outperform weaker bases (K2CO3, Cs2CO3) for hindered secondary amine substrates by driving deprotonation equilibrium.
H4 new (active, medium): Temperatures above 100°C will cause catalyst decomposition and reduced yields due to phosphine oxidation and Pd black formation, creating an optimal window at 80-100°C.
H5 new (active, low): Solvent polarity significantly affects yield: polar aprotic solvents (dioxane, toluene) will outperform coordinating solvents (DMF, DMSO) by preventing catalyst inhibition.
